config.c(3) Library Functions Manual config.c(3)

NAME

config.c - 📋 TOML configuration file parser with schema validation and CLI override support

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


union option_parsed_value_t
Union holding all possible parsed option values. struct option_type_handler_t
Type handler - encapsulates all 4 operations for one option type. struct config_builder_t
Helper structure for building config content in a buffer.

Macros

Internal Macros



#define CONFIG_WARN(fmt, ...)
Print configuration warning using the logging system. #define CONFIG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)
Print configuration debug message.

Functions


asciichat_error_t config_load_and_apply (asciichat_mode_t detected_mode, const char *config_path, bool strict, options_t *opts)
Main function to load configuration from file and apply to global options. asciichat_error_t config_create_default (const char *config_path)
Create default configuration file with all default values. asciichat_error_t config_load_system_and_user (asciichat_mode_t detected_mode, bool strict, options_t *opts)
Load system config first, then user config (user config overrides system).

TOML Value Extraction Helpers
Helper functions to safely extract typed values from TOML datum structures.



Schema-Based Configuration Parser



Detailed Description

📋 TOML configuration file parser with schema validation and CLI override support

Definition in file config.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define CONFIG_DEBUG( fmt, ...)

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  /* Debug messages are only shown in debug builds after logging is initialized */                                   \


  /* Use log_debug which safely checks initialization itself */                                                      \


  log_debug(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                                                     \
} while (0)




Print configuration debug message. Debug messages use the logging
    system if it's initialized, otherwise they are silently dropped.


Definition at line 56 of file config.c.


56 #define CONFIG_DEBUG(fmt, ...)                                                                                         \
57   do {                                                                                                                 \
58     /* Debug messages are only shown in debug builds after logging is initialized */                                   \
59     /* Use log_debug which safely checks initialization itself */                                                      \
60     log_debug(fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                                                     \
61   } while (0)






Referenced by config_load_and_apply(), and
    config_load_system_and_user().






#define
  CONFIG_WARN( fmt, ...)


Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  log_warn("Config file: " fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                                      \
} while (0)






Print configuration warning using the logging system. Uses
    log_warn so that config warnings respect the --quiet flag and are routed
    through the logging system for proper filtering.


Definition at line 45 of file config.c.


45 #define CONFIG_WARN(fmt, ...)                                                                                          \
46   do {                                                                                                                 \
47     log_warn("Config file: " fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                                                      \
48   } while (0)






Referenced by config_load_and_apply().
